pkgmetadata:
orsonteodoro@hotmail.com Orson Teodoro
<!--
Clean up for fresh install:
killall -9 psql /etc/init.d/lobe-chat stop /etc/init.d/lobehub stop /etc/init.d/postgresql-17 stop
rm -rf /var/lib/postgresql/17/data
or
killall -9 psql /etc/init.d/lobe-chat stop /etc/init.d/lobehub stop /etc/init.d/postgresql-17 stop
# Login the PostgreSQL server psql -p 5432 -U postgres
# Drop user lobehub_user REASSIGN OWNED BY lobehub_user TO postgres; DROP OWNED BY lobehub_user; SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE usename = 'lobehub_user'; REVOKE ALL PRIVILEGES ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA public FROM lobehub_user; DROP USER IF EXISTS lobehub_user;
# Drop the lobehub database DROP DATABASE lobehub WITH (FORCE);
Install PostgreSQL:
emerge -1vuDN dev-db/postgresql:17
Configure PostgreSQL:
emerge --config dev-db/postgresql:17
Start PostgreSQL server:
/etc/init.d/postgresql-17 start
Create a new user for LobeHub:
psql -p 5432 -U postgres CREATE ROLE lobehub_user WITH LOGIN PASSWORD 'your_very_strong_password_here'; CREATE DATABASE lobehub; ALTER DATABASE lobehub OWNER TO lobehub_user;
Allow the user to create extension:
ALTER ROLE lobehub_user CREATEDB;
Install pgvector for RAG and embeddings support:
emerge -1vuDN dev-db/pgvector
Create extension:
psql -p 5432 -U postgres ALTER ROLE lobehub_user WITH SUPERUSER; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;
Run `emerge LobeHub` to create the database tables in the lobehub database.
Revoke the superuser privilege after emerge.
psql -p 5432 -U postgres ALTER ROLE lobehub_user WITH NOSUPERUSER;
Update /etc/conf.d/lobehub with the port and username and password changes.
To fix psql login as password, change from trust to md5 in pg_hba.conf
# IPv4 local connections: host all all 127.0.0.1/32 md5 # IPv6 local connections: host all all ::1/128 md5
psql -p 5432 -U postgres SHOW hba_file; nano /etc/postgresql-17/pg_hba.conf
Additional configuration notes:
Use the least privileges or limited user for the database so use lobehub_user in DATABASE_URI.
Do not use postgres for DATABASE_URI. The postgres user is like root.
The localhost and 127.0.0.1 identifiers are mutually exclusive. Make sure it is the same in both /etc/conf.d/lobehub and in the remote OAuth settings.
Integration with GitHub OAuth as the login provider was tested successfully.
The e-mail based login was not tested yet for this ebuild.
-->
https://github.com/lobehub/lobehub/issues https://github.com/lobehub/lobehub/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md https://lobehub.com/docs/usage/start
Use Ceph as a S3 compatible locally hosted storage.
Build the client frontend to interact with the self-hosted server or the official LobeHub service.
Note: For complete self-hosting enable also the pwa USE flag.
Add dependencies to support embeddings.
Add support for file management.
Use MinIO as S3 compatible locally hosted storage.
Store information server-side in PostgresDB.
Build both the PWA (Progressive Web App) for use in a web browser and the self-hosting server.
Add basic online support by default.
Add dependencies to support RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) to allow the AI to give current and up-to-date information or answers with less hallucinations.
Add support for cache optimization with Redis.
Use S3 remote hosted storage.
Add self-hosted SearXNG dependency for partial Web RAG support (summaries or web links).
Install command line tools referenced in the marketplace.
It will install the following:
@lobehub/cli - manage and connect to LobeHub services, install marketplace skills (verified) @lobehub/market-cli - device registration and auth management, install marketplace skills (unverified)
